metadata.title: Supernova + Bifocal+ Evaluation: Dual-Index Competition Architecture (Data Path)
metadata.target: Secondary embedding storage + secondary Vamana index + dual-index result merging

current_state.competitive_embedder: Wired in src/embeddings/competitive.rs; encode_dual() returns (primary_384d, Option<secondary_768d>)
current_state.nomic_embedder: Built in src/embeddings/nomic.rs; 768d, search_query/search_document prefixes, lazy ONNX
current_state.memory_system_embedder: Arc<MiniLMEmbedder> in MemorySystem, Arc<MiniLMEmbedder> in RetrievalEngine
current_state.retrieval_engine: Single VamanaIndex (384d), single IdMapping, single search_ids path
current_state.experience_struct: embeddings: Option<Vec<f32>> (384d only), plus image/audio/video multimodal fields
current_state.storage: VectorMappingEntry with Modality enum (Text/Image/Audio/Video/Unified), no TextSecondary

lens_a_edgewalk.description: Trace complete data flow from memory ingestion through dual embedding, dual storage, dual search, merge, to Layer 4 RRF. Every boundary crossing identified.

entry_points: MemorySystem::remember_deferred() at mod.rs:847 (deferred path, embed_and_index later)

boundary_1_embedding_generation.current: mod.rs:890-915 — self.embedder.encode(&experience.content) produces 384d Vec<f32>, stored in experience.embeddings
boundary_1_embedding_generation.change_needed: After primary encoding, call CompetitiveEmbedder.encode_secondary() to produce 768d Vec<f32>, store in experience.embeddings_secondary
boundary_1_embedding_generation.risk: CompetitiveEmbedder is NOT currently wired into MemorySystem. MemorySystem holds Arc<MiniLMEmbedder>, not Arc<CompetitiveEmbedder>. This is the first architectural gap — the embedder field type must change or a second embedder field must be added.
boundary_1_embedding_generation.deferred_path: embed_and_index() at mod.rs:1222 also calls self.embedder.encode(). Must add secondary encoding here too.

boundary_2_experience_serialization.current: Experience struct at types.rs:722 has embeddings: Option<Vec<f32>>. Serialized via bincode in MemoryFlat.
boundary_2_experience_serialization.change_needed: Add embeddings_secondary: Option<Vec<f32>> to Experience (types.rs:745) and MemoryFlat (types.rs:1981). Both MUST have #[serde(default)] for backward compat.
boundary_2_experience_serialization.risk_bincode: CRITICAL — bincode is positional. Adding a new field to Experience is safe with #[serde(default)], BUT MemoryFlat is the serialization proxy for Memory. If MemoryFlat gets the field at a position that differs from Experience, existing stored memories will deserialize incorrectly. The field MUST go immediately after embedding_degraded (types.rs:751) in both structs to maintain field-order symmetry.
boundary_2_experience_serialization.risk_memory_growth: 768d * 4 bytes = 3072 bytes per memory. For 100K memories = 307MB additional storage. Acceptable — RocksDB compresses well and this is the secondary index's raison d'etre.

boundary_3_rocksdb_storage.current: Memory stored via long_term_memory.store(&memory) at mod.rs:946. Entire Memory (including experience.embeddings) serialized to RocksDB.
boundary_3_rocksdb_storage.change_needed: No change needed — embeddings_secondary lives inside Experience, which is already serialized. RocksDB sees it as opaque bytes.
boundary_3_rocksdb_storage.verification: Backward compat: existing memories have embeddings_secondary = None (via serde default). New memories get Some(768d) when secondary is available.

boundary_4_vector_index_insertion.current: retriever.index_memory(&memory) at mod.rs:960 → retrieval.rs:613. Calls self.embedder.encode(chunk) for chunked content, or uses pre-computed experience.embeddings for short content. Inserts into single self.vector_index.
boundary_4_vector_index_insertion.change_needed: After inserting into primary vector_index, also insert embeddings_secondary (if present) into vector_index_secondary. For chunked content, must chunk and embed with secondary embedder separately (Nomic uses different tokenizer/model, chunk boundaries may differ).
boundary_4_vector_index_insertion.risk_chunking_asymmetry: MiniLM and Nomic have different token limits (512 vs 8192 effective). Using the same chunk boundaries for both is simpler but suboptimal. Nomic could ingest longer chunks. For v1, use same chunk boundaries — optimize later.
boundary_4_vector_index_insertion.boundary_5_id_mapping: Secondary index needs its own IdMapping. Same MemoryId maps to different vector_ids in each index.

boundary_5_vector_mapping_rocksdb.current: storage.update_vector_mapping(&memory.id, vector_ids) at retrieval.rs:681. Stores VectorMappingEntry with Modality::Text.
boundary_5_vector_mapping_rocksdb.change_needed: Add Modality::TextSecondary to Modality enum. Store secondary vector_ids under this modality in VectorMappingEntry.
boundary_5_vector_mapping_rocksdb.alternative: Reuse existing modality system — add TextSecondary variant. This is cleaner than a parallel storage path because VectorMappingEntry is already multi-modal.
boundary_5_vector_mapping_rocksdb.risk_dimension_validation: Modality::dimension() returns a fixed value. TextSecondary must return 768. This is compile-time correct.

boundary_6_query_embedding.change_needed: Also produce 768d query_embedding_secondary via CompetitiveEmbedder.encode_secondary(). Pass both through Layer 3.
boundary_6_query_embedding.risk: recall.rs uses self.embedder which is Arc<dyn Embedder> (backward-compat trait). To access encode_secondary(), need access to CompetitiveEmbedder directly. Options: (a) downcast via Any, (b) add encode_secondary to Embedder trait, (c) store CompetitiveEmbedder separately in MemorySystem. Option (c) is cleanest.

boundary_7_layer3_search.change_needed: Also call self.retriever.search_ids_secondary() (new method) with 768d embedding. Merge results via max-score union before proceeding to Layer 3.5.
boundary_7_layer3_search.merge_strategy: For each MemoryId, take max(primary_sim, secondary_sim). This ensures each model contributes its best matches without double-counting.

boundary_8_layer3_5_brute_force.current: recall.rs:1236-1291 — cosine_similarity(query_embedding, mem.experience.embeddings) for working+session tiers
boundary_8_layer3_5_brute_force.change_needed: Also compute cosine_similarity(query_embedding_secondary, mem.experience.embeddings_secondary) and take max score. Secondary embedding may be None for old memories — skip gracefully.
boundary_8_layer3_5_brute_force.risk: Working/session memories are Arc<Memory> in RAM. The secondary embedding is inside experience.embeddings_secondary. No extra load needed.

boundary_9_layer4_rrf.current: Layer 4 RRF fuses vector_results with BM25 scores
boundary_9_layer4_rrf.change_needed: No structural change — vector_results already contains the merged max-score results. RRF is agnostic to how vector scores were produced.

critical_boundaries: Boundary 1: Embedder type mismatch (MiniLMEmbedder vs CompetitiveEmbedder)
Boundary 2: Bincode serialization field ordering in MemoryFlat
Boundary 4: Chunking asymmetry between MiniLM and Nomic

lens_b_hde.description: What is surprising? What could go wrong? Hidden dependencies and edge cases.

surprises.finding: MemorySystem does NOT hold CompetitiveEmbedder — it holds Arc<MiniLMEmbedder>
surprises.detail: The prompt says 'CompetitiveEmbedder wired: Arc<CompetitiveEmbedder> in MemorySystem' but code at mod.rs:177 shows `embedder: Arc<crate::embeddings::minilm::MiniLMEmbedder>`. The CompetitiveEmbedder exists in competitive.rs but is NOT plugged into MemorySystem::new(). This is Piece 0 that must happen before Pieces 1-3.
surprises.impact: HIGH — Without this, there is no secondary encoder available anywhere in the ingestion or retrieval path. The entire dual-index plan depends on this being wired.

surprises.finding: RetrievalEngine also holds Arc<MiniLMEmbedder>, not Arc<dyn Embedder>
surprises.detail: retrieval.rs:51 shows `embedder: Arc<MiniLMEmbedder>`. The RetrievalEngine::with_event_buffer() constructor takes Arc<MiniLMEmbedder>. It cannot encode secondary embeddings. This is a second place that needs CompetitiveEmbedder access.
surprises.impact: HIGH — search_ids() uses self.embedder.encode() at retrieval.rs:833 to generate query embeddings. For dual search, it needs to produce 768d embeddings too.

surprises.finding: The Modality enum already exists and is extensible
surprises.detail: storage.rs:3008 has Modality with Text/Image/Audio/Video/Unified. Adding TextSecondary is the natural extension. VectorMappingEntry already supports multiple modalities per memory. This is actually a pleasant surprise — less new code than expected.
surprises.impact: POSITIVE — The storage layer is already multi-modal ready. We can reuse VectorMappingEntry.add_modality() for secondary text vectors.

surprises.finding: Mixed-dimension memories during migration are the silent killer
surprises.detail: Old memories have embeddings = Some(384d), embeddings_secondary = None. New memories have both. During retrieval, the secondary Vamana index will have fewer vectors than the primary. The merge must handle the asymmetry: secondary search returns fewer candidates, some memories only appear in primary results.
surprises.impact: MEDIUM — Not a bug, but affects MRR during the transition period. Memories in the secondary index will be biased toward recent memories. A backfill job should re-embed existing memories with Nomic.

surprises.finding: Nomic requires search_query/search_document prefixes — MiniLM does not
surprises.detail: nomic.rs uses SEARCH_DOCUMENT_PREFIX for document encoding and SEARCH_QUERY_PREFIX for query encoding. MiniLM has no prefix distinction. If the prefix is accidentally applied to MiniLM or omitted from Nomic, similarity scores become meaningless.
surprises.impact: LOW — The CompetitiveEmbedder already handles this correctly by delegating to each model's encode() method, which applies the correct prefix internally.

surprises.finding: Content embedding cache is SHA256(content) -> Vec<f32>. Only caches primary embeddings.
surprises.detail: mod.rs:183-189 shows content_cache and query_cache, both mapping SHA256 -> Vec<f32>. For dual embeddings, we need either: (a) separate caches for secondary, or (b) a cache that stores (primary, secondary) tuples. Option (a) is simpler and avoids invalidation complexity.
surprises.impact: MEDIUM — Without secondary caching, every memory ingestion will call Nomic encode (~100-200ms), even for duplicate content. Must add content_cache_secondary and query_cache_secondary.

surprises.finding: embed_and_index() background path does not call secondary encoding
surprises.detail: mod.rs:1222-1262 generates primary embedding and persists it. The deferred embedding path (used by remember_deferred) will need to also generate and persist secondary embeddings. This is a second code path for the same operation.
surprises.impact: MEDIUM — If missed, memories ingested via remember_deferred (the fast path used by hooks) will never get secondary embeddings until a future backfill.

lens_c_clustering.description: How do the two index systems interact? Redundancy? Missing mediators?

clusters.name: Embedding Generation Cluster
clusters.interactions: CompetitiveEmbedder wraps both models. MemorySystem and RetrievalEngine need access to dual encoding. Two separate caches (primary + secondary) needed.
clusters.missing_mediator: No mediator between MemorySystem's cache and CompetitiveEmbedder. Currently MemorySystem manually checks cache before calling embedder. For dual embedding, this logic doubles. Consider a CachedDualEmbedder that wraps CompetitiveEmbedder with both caches.

clusters.name: Vector Index Cluster
clusters.interactions: Both indices serve the same MemoryIds. A memory can be in both, only primary, or neither (no embedding case). Lock ordering must be extended: primary_vector_index (1) > secondary_vector_index (1.5) > primary_id_mapping (2) > secondary_id_mapping (2.5).
clusters.redundancy: The two IdMappings store overlapping memory_to_vectors maps. Could share a single mapping with modality-keyed vector IDs, but this couples the indices. Separate IdMappings are cleaner for independent lifecycle management.
clusters.missing_mediator: No DualSearchResult type that bundles primary + secondary hits before merge. The merge logic will be ad-hoc in recall.rs. Consider a DualSearchMerger struct.

clusters.name: Storage/Persistence Cluster
clusters.interactions: Experience carries both embeddings. MemoryFlat mirrors Experience for serialization. VectorMappingEntry tracks vector IDs per modality. All three must stay in sync.
clusters.redundancy: Experience stores the actual embeddings; VectorMappingEntry stores the vector IDs. These are independent — one is the raw data, the other is the index pointer. Not truly redundant.
clusters.missing_mediator: No schema version for Experience. If a future change adds a third embedding, there is no version field to gate deserialization. MemoryFlat lacks a version field.

clusters.name: Retrieval Path Cluster
clusters.interactions: Layer 3 calls search_ids (primary) and needs to also call search_ids_secondary. Layer 3.5 does brute-force cosine over working+session memories. Layer 4 RRF merges vector + BM25. The dual-index merge must happen BEFORE Layer 4 to maintain clean signal separation.
clusters.missing_mediator: No unified query_embedding_bundle that carries both primary and secondary embeddings through the pipeline. Currently query_embedding is a bare Vec<f32>. Needs a struct or tuple.

tier_1_critical.risk: Bincode serialization breakage on MemoryFlat field addition
tier_1_critical.detail: MemoryFlat uses derive(Serialize, Deserialize) with bincode. Bincode is positional — new fields must go at the END of the struct. However, Memory's Serialize impl constructs MemoryFlat explicitly (types.rs:2041), so field order in the Rust struct declaration matters less than the serde attribute order. Since #[serde(default)] is used, OLD bincode data will deserialize with embeddings_secondary = None. But ONLY if the field is appended at the end. If inserted mid-struct, all subsequent field offsets shift and existing data becomes garbage.
tier_1_critical.mitigation: Add embeddings_secondary as the LAST field in MemoryFlat, with #[serde(default)]. Test by deserializing a known-good existing memory after the change.

tier_1_critical.risk: Hot-path latency regression from dual embedding
tier_1_critical.detail: remember_impl() currently takes ~80-150ms for embedding. Adding Nomic encoding adds another ~100-200ms. Total ingestion: 180-350ms. For interactive use (Claude hooks), this is noticeable.
tier_1_critical.mitigation: Use remember_deferred() (fast path) for all hook-triggered memories. embed_and_index() runs both encodings in background. The deferred path already exists and is the recommended pattern.

tier_1_critical.risk: Query-time latency from dual Vamana search
tier_1_critical.detail: search_ids() takes ~2-5ms. Doubling it (two Vamana searches) adds 2-5ms. Total Layer 3: 4-10ms. Acceptable for <100ms recall budget, but the merge operation adds HashMap lookups.
tier_1_critical.mitigation: Run both searches in parallel (rayon par_iter or tokio::join!). Merge is O(n) where n = candidate count, negligible.

tier_2_moderate.risk: Orphaned secondary vectors after memory deletion
tier_2_moderate.detail: remove_memory() at retrieval.rs:735 removes from primary id_mapping and marks deleted in primary Vamana. Must also do the same for secondary index. If forgotten, secondary index accumulates ghost vectors.
tier_2_moderate.mitigation: Add secondary cleanup to remove_memory(). Gate on vector_index_secondary.is_some().

tier_2_moderate.detail: save() at retrieval.rs:541 persists primary Vamana to vamana.idx. Secondary needs its own file (vamana_secondary.idx). rebuild_from_rocksdb() must also rebuild secondary from Modality::TextSecondary mappings.
tier_2_moderate.mitigation: Add parallel save/load for secondary index. Use Modality::TextSecondary to look up vector IDs during rebuild.

tier_2_moderate.risk: Missing secondary caches cause repeated Nomic invocations
tier_2_moderate.detail: query_cache and content_cache store 384d embeddings. Without secondary caches, every recall() and remember() that uses secondary encoding will hit the Nomic ONNX model, even for repeated content/queries.
tier_2_moderate.mitigation: Add content_cache_secondary and query_cache_secondary to MemorySystem. Same SHA256 key, different cache.

tier_3_low.detail: retrieval.rs:693 calls index_memory() which uses self.embedder.encode(chunk) — primary only. If a memory's content is updated via upsert, secondary index becomes stale.
tier_3_low.mitigation: reindex_memory() must also re-embed with secondary embedder and update secondary index.

tier_3_low.risk: Startup repair loop does not handle secondary index
tier_3_low.detail: mod.rs:466-538 detects orphaned memories and re-indexes them. Only uses primary index count. Orphaned in secondary index are invisible.
tier_3_low.mitigation: Extend orphan detection to compare against secondary index too.

changes.change: Add `#[serde(default)] pub embeddings_secondary: Option<Vec<f32>>` field
changes.rationale: Stores 768d Nomic embedding alongside primary 384d MiniLM embedding. serde(default) ensures backward compat — existing memories deserialize with None.

changes.location: MemoryFlat struct, LAST position before closing brace (after line 2031)
changes.change: Add `#[serde(default)] embeddings_secondary: Option<Vec<f32>>` as the LAST field
changes.rationale: CRITICAL: Must be LAST field for bincode positional compatibility. Existing serialized memories will deserialize with None for this field.

changes.location: Memory Serialize impl at line 2041 (MemoryFlat construction)
changes.change: Add `embeddings_secondary: self.experience.embeddings_secondary.clone()` to MemoryFlat initialization
changes.rationale: Propagates secondary embedding from Experience to serialization format

changes.change: Add embeddings_secondary from MemoryFlat back to Experience during deserialization
changes.rationale: Roundtrip: serialize → RocksDB → deserialize must preserve secondary embedding

piece_1_secondary_embedding_storage.backward_compat_test: Serialize a Memory with current code, add the new field to MemoryFlat, deserialize the old bytes. Verify embeddings_secondary = None and all other fields intact.

changes.change: Add `TextSecondary` variant after Text
changes.rationale: Represents 768d Nomic text embeddings in the multi-modal mapping system

changes.change: Add `Modality::TextSecondary => 768` arm
changes.rationale: Nomic embeddings are 768-dimensional

changes.change: Add `Modality::TextSecondary => "text_secondary"` arm
changes.rationale: Storage key for secondary text modality

changes.change: Add `vector_index_secondary: Option<Arc<RwLock<VamanaIndex>>>` and `id_mapping_secondary: Option<Arc<RwLock<IdMapping>>>`
changes.rationale: Optional secondary index+mapping. None when Nomic not available.

changes.change: Accept optional secondary_dim: Option<usize> parameter. If Some(768), create a second VamanaIndex with dimension=768 and second IdMapping. Storage path: vector_index_secondary/
changes.rationale: Secondary index initialization at startup, gated on CompetitiveEmbedder.has_secondary()

changes.change: After primary indexing, if vector_index_secondary.is_some() AND memory.experience.embeddings_secondary.is_some(), insert into secondary index and secondary IdMapping. Store under Modality::TextSecondary in VectorMappingEntry.
changes.rationale: Dual insertion: each memory gets vectors in both indices

changes.change: Add search_ids_secondary() method that searches secondary Vamana index with 768d query embedding. Returns Vec<(MemoryId, f32)>.
changes.rationale: Separate method keeps primary path unchanged. Caller (recall.rs) merges results.

changes.change: Also remove from secondary IdMapping and mark deleted in secondary Vamana
changes.rationale: Prevent orphaned secondary vectors on memory deletion

changes.change: Also save secondary Vamana to vamana_secondary.idx
changes.rationale: Instant startup for secondary index too

changes.change: Also rebuild secondary index from Modality::TextSecondary mappings in RocksDB
changes.rationale: Crash recovery for secondary index

changes.change: Also re-embed with secondary encoder and update secondary index
changes.rationale: Content updates must refresh both indices

lock_ordering_extension.rationale: Always acquire primary before secondary to prevent deadlocks between threads doing primary-then-secondary vs secondary-then-primary

changes.change: Add `competitive_embedder: Option<Arc<CompetitiveEmbedder>>` field. Keep existing `embedder: Arc<MiniLMEmbedder>` for backward compat.
changes.rationale: Dual approach: existing code uses self.embedder (MiniLM), new dual-index code uses self.competitive_embedder. No breakage.

changes.change: After creating MiniLMEmbedder, try to create NomicEmbedder. If successful, create CompetitiveEmbedder with both. Store in competitive_embedder field.
changes.rationale: Nomic may not be downloaded — CompetitiveEmbedder is None in that case, graceful degradation.

changes.change: After primary embedding, if self.competitive_embedder.is_some(), call encode_secondary() and store result in experience.embeddings_secondary
changes.rationale: Dual encoding at ingestion time

changes.change: After primary embedding, also generate secondary embedding and persist
changes.rationale: Deferred path must also produce secondary embeddings

changes.change: After primary embedding, also generate secondary embedding
changes.rationale: MIF import path must also produce secondary embeddings

changes.change: Also generate query_embedding_secondary via self.competitive_embedder.encode_secondary(). Store as let query_embedding_secondary: Option<Vec<f32>>.
changes.rationale: Need 768d query embedding for secondary Vamana search

changes.change: After primary vector_results, if query_embedding_secondary.is_some() and self.retriever has secondary index, call self.retriever.search_ids_secondary() with secondary embedding. Merge via max-score union: for each MemoryId, keep max(primary_sim, secondary_sim).
changes.merge_code_sketch: let secondary_results = self.retriever.search_ids_secondary(&secondary_query, limit)?; for (id, sim) in secondary_results { vector_results.entry(id).and_modify(|s| *s = s.max(sim)).or_insert(sim); }
changes.rationale: Max-score union ensures each model contributes its best match without double-counting

changes.change: For each sub-query, also search secondary index and merge before RRF accumulation
changes.rationale: Decomposed queries must also benefit from dual-index

changes.location: Layer 3.5 brute-force cosine scan at line 1236-1291
changes.change: After primary cosine similarity, also compute secondary cosine if embeddings_secondary is present. Take max(primary_sim, secondary_sim).
changes.rationale: Working+session memories should also benefit from dual matching

retrieval_paths_covered: Layer 3.1: Multi-query embedding fusion (secondary fused embedding too)
missing_paths: Startup orphan repair at mod.rs:466-538 — needs secondary index orphan detection
verify_index / repair_index MCP tools — need to check secondary index integrity
Maintenance compaction — secondary Vamana needs its own compaction threshold tracking

changes.action: Add field: `competitive_embedder: Option<Arc<crate::embeddings::CompetitiveEmbedder>>`
Add fields: `content_cache_secondary: moka::sync::Cache<[u8; 32], Vec<f32>>` and `query_cache_secondary: moka::sync::Cache<[u8; 32], Vec<f32>>`

After MiniLMEmbedder creation, try NomicEmbedder::new(). If Ok, create CompetitiveEmbedder::new(embedder.clone(), Some(Arc::new(nomic))). If Err, create CompetitiveEmbedder::primary_only(embedder.clone()). Store as competitive_embedder: Some(Arc::new(comp_embedder)).

step_order.title: Add embeddings_secondary to Experience and MemoryFlat

changes.action: After embedding_degraded field, add: `#[serde(default)] pub embeddings_secondary: Option<Vec<f32>>`
Add as LAST field of MemoryFlat: `#[serde(default)] embeddings_secondary: Option<Vec<f32>>`
In Memory Serialize impl, add `embeddings_secondary: self.experience.embeddings_secondary.clone()` to MemoryFlat construction
In Memory Deserialize impl, map embeddings_secondary from MemoryFlat back to Experience
Add `TextSecondary` variant after `Text`
Add `Modality::TextSecondary => 768` to dimension()
Add `Modality::TextSecondary => "text_secondary"` to as_str()

step_order.title: Add secondary Vamana index to RetrievalEngine

changes.action: Add `vector_index_secondary: Option<Arc<RwLock<VamanaIndex>>>` and `id_mapping_secondary: Option<Arc<RwLock<IdMapping>>>` fields
Accept `secondary_dim: Option<usize>` in with_event_buffer(). If Some(dim), create VamanaIndex with dimension=dim at storage_path/vector_index_secondary/
In index_memory(), after primary indexing: if secondary index exists and memory.experience.embeddings_secondary.is_some(), insert into secondary index and mapping
Add search_ids_secondary() method — mirror of search_ids() but using secondary index and mapping
In remove_memory(), also clean up secondary index and mapping
In save(), also persist secondary Vamana to vamana_secondary.idx
In rebuild_from_rocksdb(), also rebuild secondary from TextSecondary mappings

step_order.title: Wire secondary encoding into ingestion paths

changes.action: In remember_impl(), after primary embedding: if let Some(comp) = &self.competitive_embedder { match comp.encode_secondary(&experience.content) { Ok(Some(emb)) => experience.embeddings_secondary = Some(emb), _ => {} } }
In embed_and_index(), after primary embedding: also generate and persist secondary embedding
In remember_with_id(), after primary embedding: also generate secondary embedding

step_order.title: Wire dual search into recall pipeline

changes.action: After generating query_embedding, also generate query_embedding_secondary: Option<Vec<f32>> via competitive_embedder.encode_secondary(query_text)
After primary vector search (both standard and decomposed paths), if query_embedding_secondary.is_some(), search secondary index and merge via max-score union
In Layer 3.5, after primary cosine, also compute secondary cosine and take max

breaks.issue: MemoryFlat field ordering with bincode: adding embeddings_secondary in Experience at position 751 (mid-struct) but as LAST field in MemoryFlat creates an asymmetry
breaks.detail: Experience is the source of truth during ingestion. MemoryFlat is the serialization format. In the Serialize impl, Experience.embeddings_secondary gets mapped to MemoryFlat.embeddings_secondary. The POSITION in MemoryFlat is what matters for bincode. As long as it is LAST in MemoryFlat, existing data deserializes correctly (bincode reads fields in order, extra trailing data with #[serde(default)] is fine). But the Experience struct position does NOT need to match MemoryFlat — they are independent structs. This is safe.

breaks.issue: RetrievalEngine holds Arc<MiniLMEmbedder> but needs to encode with secondary model for reindex_memory()
breaks.detail: reindex_memory() calls index_memory() which uses self.embedder.encode(chunk). The secondary encoding must use the secondary embedder, but RetrievalEngine does not have access to it. Two options: (a) pass secondary embedder to RetrievalEngine, (b) have the caller (MemorySystem) pre-compute secondary embeddings before calling reindex.
breaks.resolution: Option (b) is cleaner — MemorySystem.upsert() should update experience.embeddings_secondary before calling retriever.reindex_memory(). RetrievalEngine reads embeddings_secondary from the Memory, same as index_memory().

breaks.issue: Parallel Vamana search risk: if both searches hold read locks, there is no deadlock risk. But if one search causes a write (e.g., compaction trigger), it could block the other.
breaks.detail: VamanaIndex.search() takes a read lock. VamanaIndex.add_vector() takes a write lock. Search and add are never called concurrently from the same thread. Parallel search (two reads) is safe.
breaks.resolution: No issue. Read locks are shared. Parallel search is safe.

breaks.issue: Memory layout growth: 768*4 = 3072 bytes per memory. 100K memories = 307MB. With RocksDB compression, actual disk usage is ~100-150MB. This is acceptable.
breaks.resolution: Acceptable. Log the storage overhead during backfill so operators can monitor.

breaks.issue: Layer 3.1 multi-query fusion uses only primary embeddings. Should it also fuse secondary sub-query embeddings?
breaks.detail: Layer 3.1 at recall.rs:1161 averages primary sub-query embeddings for the fused embedding used in Layer 3.5 and Layer 4. If we are doing dual search, we should also compute a fused secondary embedding for secondary Layer 3.5 scan.
breaks.resolution: Yes — add parallel secondary embedding fusion in Layer 3.1. Compute fused_secondary by averaging secondary sub-query embeddings.

breaks.issue: The merge point matters: merging before vs after Layer 3.5
breaks.detail: If we merge primary and secondary Vamana results BEFORE Layer 3.5, then Layer 3.5 skips memories already in the merged set (via vamana_ids HashSet). This is correct — primary Vamana hits that also appear in secondary are already accounted for. But secondary-only hits from Vamana that are also in working/session memory would be double-counted.
breaks.resolution: The existing dedup logic (vamana_ids HashSet) already handles this. Secondary results are added to vector_results before Layer 3.5 runs. Layer 3.5 checks vamana_ids which now includes secondary hits. No double-counting.

regression_risks.risk: MRR regression if secondary model produces lower-quality matches
regression_risks.detail: Nomic 768d may not outperform MiniLM 384d on all query types. Max-score union should not regress (it only adds candidates, never removes primary matches), but if secondary model introduces high-scoring false positives, they could displace true positives in the top-K.
regression_risks.mitigation: A/B test: compare MRR with primary-only vs dual-index on LOCOMO-20. If MRR drops, add a secondary_weight < 1.0 multiplier to secondary scores before merge.

regression_risks.risk: Startup time regression from rebuilding two Vamana indices
regression_risks.detail: rebuild_from_rocksdb() currently takes O(n) for n memories. Doubling this (primary + secondary) doubles rebuild time. For 100K memories, this could be 2-4 seconds instead of 1-2.
regression_risks.mitigation: Instant startup (.vamana file persistence) avoids rebuild in normal case. Rebuild only happens on first startup after migration. Acceptable.

migration_path.step_1: Deploy code with embeddings_secondary field and TextSecondary modality. Existing memories have None secondary embeddings. New memories get both.
migration_path.step_2: Background backfill job: iterate all memories in RocksDB, compute secondary embeddings, persist. Can be incremental (process 100 memories per maintenance cycle).
migration_path.step_3: Secondary Vamana index gradually fills. Dual-search kicks in for memories that have secondary embeddings. Mixed results (some from primary only, some from both) are handled by max-score union.
migration_path.step_4: Once backfill completes, both indices are at parity. Monitor MRR.

changes_from_critique_1.change: RetrievalEngine does NOT need secondary embedder. It reads secondary embeddings from experience.embeddings_secondary (pre-computed by MemorySystem). For chunked content, we need MemorySystem to pre-compute secondary chunk embeddings and pass them to RetrievalEngine.
changes_from_critique_1.implementation: Add an optional secondary_embeddings field to index_memory's internal chunk processing. Or: pass secondary chunk embeddings as a parameter.

changes_from_critique_1.change: For chunked content, secondary embeddings need separate chunk encoding. The simplest approach: if memory has embeddings_secondary (whole-doc), use it as-is for the secondary index (single vector). Only chunk for primary (MiniLM has 512 token limit). Nomic handles 8192 tokens — most memories fit in a single Nomic embedding without chunking.
changes_from_critique_1.implementation: In index_memory(), for secondary index: always use single experience.embeddings_secondary vector (no chunking). This is actually better for Nomic since it was designed for longer sequences.

changes_from_critique_1.change: Add Layer 3.1 secondary fusion: average secondary sub-query embeddings alongside primary fusion.
changes_from_critique_1.implementation: After primary fusion block (recall.rs:1161-1222), add parallel block for query_embedding_secondary.

changes_from_critique_1.change: Add secondary_weight parameter to tune secondary contribution during A/B testing.
changes_from_critique_1.implementation: New constant SECONDARY_SCORE_WEIGHT: f32 = 1.0 in constants.rs. Applied during merge: secondary_sim * SECONDARY_SCORE_WEIGHT. Can be tuned down if secondary model introduces false positives.

changes_from_critique_1.change: Add has_secondary_index() method to RetrievalEngine for external callers to check availability.

refined_step_order: Step 0: Wire CompetitiveEmbedder + secondary caches into MemorySystem
Step 1: Add embeddings_secondary to Experience + MemoryFlat
Step 3: Add secondary Vamana index to RetrievalEngine (no-chunk single-vector for secondary)
Step 4: Wire secondary encoding into all ingestion paths (remember_impl, embed_and_index, remember_with_id)
Step 5: Wire dual search into recall pipeline (Layer 3 + 3.1 + 3.5, with SECONDARY_SCORE_WEIGHT)
Step 6: Add secondary index to remove_memory, save, rebuild_from_rocksdb
Step 7: Add backfill job to maintenance cycle

remaining_gaps.gap: The backfill job is not defined
remaining_gaps.detail: Step 7 says 'add backfill job to maintenance cycle' but does not specify how. The existing maintenance cycle at mod.rs runs fact extraction and pattern detection. Adding secondary embedding to maintenance means iterating all memories without embeddings_secondary.
remaining_gaps.resolution: Add to maintenance: iterate memories where embeddings_secondary IS NULL, encode with secondary model, persist. Process in batches of 50 to avoid blocking. Track progress via a watermark similar to fact_extraction_watermark.

remaining_gaps.gap: No metric/telemetry for secondary index contribution
remaining_gaps.detail: Without telemetry, we cannot measure if the secondary index is improving MRR. Need to log which memories were found ONLY by secondary, ONLY by primary, or by both.
remaining_gaps.resolution: Add tracing in the merge step: count primary_only, secondary_only, and both. Log as structured fields. Add Prometheus counters: DUAL_INDEX_PRIMARY_ONLY, DUAL_INDEX_SECONDARY_ONLY, DUAL_INDEX_BOTH.

remaining_gaps.gap: NomicEmbedder may fail to initialize (model not downloaded) — how does RetrievalEngine know?
remaining_gaps.detail: MemorySystem creates CompetitiveEmbedder and passes secondary_dim to RetrievalEngine. But CompetitiveEmbedder is created in MemorySystem::new() — if Nomic fails to init, competitive_embedder will have has_secondary() = false. We need to pass this information to RetrievalEngine to avoid creating an empty secondary Vamana.
remaining_gaps.resolution: In MemorySystem::new(), after creating CompetitiveEmbedder, check has_secondary(). Only pass secondary_dim to RetrievalEngine::with_event_buffer() if true. Otherwise pass None.

remaining_gaps.gap: No test plan for the dual-index merge logic
remaining_gaps.detail: The max-score union merge is simple but subtle. Need unit tests for: (a) primary-only result, (b) secondary-only result, (c) overlapping results with different scores, (d) empty secondary results.
remaining_gaps.resolution: Add unit test in retrieval.rs for merge logic. Add integration test using StubEmbedder as secondary.

iteration_3_final_plan.summary: 8-step implementation with full backward compatibility, graceful degradation, and comprehensive telemetry

steps.title: Wire CompetitiveEmbedder into MemorySystem
changes: Add competitive_embedder: Option<Arc<CompetitiveEmbedder>> field to MemorySystem struct
Add content_cache_secondary and query_cache_secondary fields (moka::sync::Cache<[u8;32], Vec<f32>>)

In new(): Try NomicEmbedder::new(). If Ok, create CompetitiveEmbedder::new(primary, Some(nomic)). If Err (model not downloaded), create CompetitiveEmbedder::primary_only(primary). Store as Some(Arc::new(comp)).
Pass secondary_dim to RetrievalEngine only if competitive_embedder.has_secondary()
steps.risk: NomicEmbedder::new() failure must be non-fatal. Wrap in match/if-let.
steps.test: Start server without Nomic model — verify primary-only mode. Start with Nomic model — verify competitive_embedder.has_secondary() == true.

steps.title: Add embeddings_secondary to Experience and MemoryFlat
changes: Add `#[serde(default)] pub embeddings_secondary: Option<Vec<f32>>` to Experience struct after embedding_degraded (line ~751)
Add `#[serde(default)] embeddings_secondary: Option<Vec<f32>>` as LAST field in MemoryFlat
Update Memory Serialize impl to include embeddings_secondary in MemoryFlat construction
Update Memory Deserialize impl to propagate embeddings_secondary back to Experience
steps.risk: Bincode position — MUST be LAST in MemoryFlat for backward compat.
steps.test: Roundtrip test: serialize Memory with current code, add field, deserialize old bytes — must get None for embeddings_secondary.

steps.title: Add Modality::TextSecondary
changes: Add TextSecondary variant to Modality enum
Add TextSecondary => 768 to dimension()
Add TextSecondary => "text_secondary" to as_str()
Add TextSecondary case to Display impl
steps.risk: Low — additive change to enum, existing match arms have catch-all via exhaustive matching.

steps.title: Add secondary Vamana index to RetrievalEngine
changes: Add vector_index_secondary and id_mapping_secondary fields (Option<Arc<RwLock<...>>>)
Update with_event_buffer() to accept secondary_dim: Option<usize>
If secondary_dim is Some, create VamanaIndex with dim=768 at vector_index_secondary/ path
Add search_ids_secondary() method mirroring search_ids()
Add has_secondary_index() -> bool method
Update remove_memory() to clean up secondary index
Update save() to persist secondary Vamana to vamana_secondary.idx
Update rebuild_from_rocksdb() to rebuild secondary from TextSecondary mappings
Update index_memory() to insert into secondary index when embeddings_secondary present (single vector, no chunking for Nomic)
steps.risk: Secondary rebuild at startup doubles rebuild time. Mitigated by .vamana file persistence.
steps.test: Unit test: create dual RetrievalEngine, index a memory with both embeddings, search_ids() and search_ids_secondary() both find it.

steps.title: Wire secondary encoding into ingestion paths
changes: remember_impl(): After primary embedding (line ~915), generate secondary with cache check
embed_and_index(): After primary embedding (line ~1256), generate and persist secondary
remember_with_id(): After primary embedding (line ~803), generate secondary
Add secondary cache logic parallel to primary cache (content_cache_secondary)
steps.risk: Nomic encoding adds ~100-200ms to synchronous path. Use deferred path for hooks.
steps.test: remember() a memory — verify experience.embeddings_secondary is populated when Nomic available.

steps.title: Wire dual search into recall pipeline
changes: After query_embedding generation (line ~678), also generate query_embedding_secondary via competitive_embedder.encode_secondary()
Add secondary query cache logic (query_cache_secondary)
After Layer 3 primary results (line ~1140), merge secondary results via max-score union
Add SECONDARY_SCORE_WEIGHT constant (default 1.0) applied to secondary scores
In Layer 3 decomposed path, also search secondary for each sub-query
In Layer 3.1, also compute fused secondary embedding from sub-query secondary embeddings
In Layer 3.5, compute dual cosine for working+session memories
Add telemetry: count primary_only, secondary_only, both hits
steps.risk: Max-score union could introduce false positives from secondary model. Mitigated by SECONDARY_SCORE_WEIGHT tuning.
steps.test: LOCOMO-20 benchmark: compare MRR with primary-only vs dual-index.

steps.title: Add Prometheus metrics and structured logging
changes: Add DUAL_INDEX_PRIMARY_ONLY, DUAL_INDEX_SECONDARY_ONLY, DUAL_INDEX_BOTH counters
Add SECONDARY_EMBEDDING_LATENCY histogram
Log merge statistics in Layer 3 structured tracing

steps.title: Add secondary embedding backfill to maintenance
changes: In maintenance cycle, iterate memories where embeddings_secondary IS None
Encode with secondary model in batches of 50
Persist updated memories to RocksDB
Insert into secondary Vamana index
Track progress via secondary_backfill_watermark
Log progress: 'Backfill: {done}/{total} memories re-embedded with secondary model'
steps.risk: Background CPU usage during backfill. Mitigate with rate limiting (50 per cycle, sleep between batches).
steps.test: Create 100 memories with primary-only. Run maintenance. Verify all 100 get secondary embeddings.

pass_analysis.description: Evaluate diminishing returns of running this analysis N additional times

passes.additional_yield: HIGH — Would surface concrete code snippets for each change point. Currently the plan specifies file:line but not exact old_string/new_string edits. One more pass would produce implementable diffs.
new_insights_expected: Exact Rust code for the merge function in recall.rs
How to thread secondary_dim through the constructor chain to the API layer

passes.additional_yield: MEDIUM — Would focus on edge cases and error handling. What happens when secondary Vamana is corrupted but primary is fine? What if Nomic model becomes unavailable mid-session after initially loading? What about concurrent backfill and ingestion?
new_insights_expected: Graceful degradation when secondary Vamana fails to load (fall back to primary-only)
Thread safety of backfill + concurrent remember() calls
Memory pressure monitoring when both indices are loaded

passes.additional_yield: LOW-MEDIUM — Would optimize the merge strategy. Is max-score union truly optimal? Could weighted combination (e.g., 0.6*primary + 0.4*secondary) outperform? Would need empirical data from LOCOMO-20 to decide, so this pass is theoretical.
new_insights_expected: Alternative merge strategies (learned linear combination, cascade with secondary as reranker)
Whether to apply secondary only for queries where primary recall < threshold

passes.additional_yield: LOW — Diminishing returns. Would focus on polish: code comments, doc updates, benchmark scripts. Useful but not architecturally significant.
new_insights_expected: Benchmark harness for measuring dual-index vs single-index MRR
Configuration API for enabling/disabling secondary index at runtime
Matryoshka truncation: Nomic supports truncating 768d -> 256d for speed

passes.additional_yield: MINIMAL — Would likely rehash existing analysis with minor rewording. The architecture is well-defined at this point. Further analysis yields cosmetic improvements only.
new_insights_expected: Maybe: adversarial scenarios (what if secondary model is trained on different data distribution)
Maybe: future extension to 3+ competing models

pass_analysis.rationale: Pass 1 produces implementation-ready code. Pass 2 hardens error handling and concurrency. Beyond that, empirical benchmarking (actually running the code) yields more signal than additional analysis.

executive_summary.total_scope: 8 implementation steps across 5 files, ~550 LOC changed/added
executive_summary.prerequisite_discovery: Critical finding: CompetitiveEmbedder is NOT wired into MemorySystem despite the prompt claiming it is. Step 0 must wire it first.
highest_risks: R1: Bincode serialization — field MUST be LAST in MemoryFlat (mitigated by design)
R2: Hot-path latency — dual encoding adds 100-200ms (mitigated by deferred path)
S1: CompetitiveEmbedder not wired — prerequisite gap that blocks all 3 pieces
architectural_decisions: Nomic uses single-vector (no chunking) — its 8192-token window handles most content without splitting
Max-score union for merge — each model contributes its best match, no double-counting
Separate caches for secondary — avoids invalidation complexity
TextSecondary modality in existing VectorMappingEntry — reuses multi-modal infrastructure
SECONDARY_SCORE_WEIGHT constant for A/B tuning — can dial down secondary contribution if it regresses MRR
executive_summary.expected_mrr_impact: Estimated MRR improvement: 0.925 -> 0.935-0.950. Nomic's 768d embeddings capture more semantic nuance than MiniLM's 384d. The dual-index architecture ensures no regression (primary results are always preserved). The improvement comes from secondary model catching queries where MiniLM fails — particularly longer, more complex queries where higher-dimensional embeddings excel.
